name: search_code
description: Search local code/files for a text or regex pattern and return normalized matches with path, line number, column, matched text, line text, and backend. Use this when you need to inspect where code, TODOs, imports, symbols, configuration keys, or arbitrary text appear inside a project. Each result includes read_context_args for reading a wider file slice with read_context.

pattern.description: Text or regular expression pattern to search for.

roots.description: Files or directories to search. Prefer project-relative paths such as ['src', 'tests'] rather than broad paths like ['/'].

regex.description: Whether pattern is a regular expression. Use false for literal text search.

hidden.description: Whether to include hidden files and directories when supported.

backend.description: Search backend. smart uses Python for small roots and external search for larger roots. auto tries rg, then grep, then pure Python fallback.

fallback.description: In auto mode, whether to try the next backend when a backend fails.

encoding.description: Text encoding for Python backend and grep output decoding. Usually utf-8.

max_results.description: Maximum number of matches to return. Null uses the tool default.

max_line_chars.description: Maximum characters kept from each matching line. Long lines are truncated.

context_before.description: Number of lines to include before each match. Null uses the tool default.

context_after.description: Number of lines to include after each match. Null uses the tool default.
